Ask questions first; Don't assume. Always seek clarification if you are unsure about the requirements or the context of the task. This will help ensure that you are on the right track and can deliver the expected results.

Use tools; Don't guess. Leverage available tools, libraries, and resources to assist you in completing tasks efficiently and accurately. This can include code editors, debugging tools, documentation, and online communities.

Do not repeat yourself (DRY) principle: Avoid duplicating code or logic. Instead, create reusable functions or components to promote maintainability and reduce the chances of bugs.
